assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with MIXED — both additive and reductive signals are present across the test file changes
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | MIXED | 1 test removed (test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers), 1 added (test_recommend_purls_dedup) in existing file; new test file adds 3 functions; net test count increases from 4 to 7 |' — the summary table row explicitly shows MIXED with both additive (new tests) and reductive (removed test) signals.

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the removed test function test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers as a reductive signal
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 34-35 in the 'Structural summary' section: 'tests/api/purl_recommend.rs: 4 functions in base, 4 in PR. 1 removed (test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers), 1 added (test_recommend_purls_dedup), 1 modified (test_recommend_purls_basic), 2 unchanged.' The removed test is explicitly identified.

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings note the assertion change in test_recommend_purls_basic — the PURL assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers to checking a versioned PURL without qualifiers, which the semantic assessment may identify as a relaxation contributing to the MIXED classification
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 34 identifies test_recommend_purls_basic as '1 modified' in the structural summary. The semantic assessment (lines 38-39) discusses the shift: 'coverage of removed behavior is replaced by coverage of the new behavior.' Additionally, report.md line 21 explicitly states the assertion changed: 'Verified by test_recommend_purls_basic asserting "pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12"' — showing the PURL assertion now checks a versioned PURL without qualifiers. criterion-1.md lines 27-28 confirms the assertion: 'assert_eq!(body.items[0].purl, "pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12")' (versioned, no qualifiers). The structural summary identifies this modification and the semantic assessment contextualizes it as part of the task-aligned semantic shift.

assertion_results.text: The new test file tests/api/purl_simplify.rs is recognized as additive (3 new test functions)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 35 in the structural summary: 'tests/api/purl_simplify.rs: New file with 3 test functions (inherently additive).' Also line 36: 'Net: 4 base functions -> 7 PR functions (+3 net).'

assertion_results.text: The new test function test_recommend_purls_dedup in the modified file is recognized as an additive signal
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '1 added (test_recommend_purls_dedup) in existing file' in the Test Change Classification row. Line 34 in structural summary: '1 added (test_recommend_purls_dedup)'. This is explicitly recognized as additive.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report/criterion files (report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md). These are verification report outputs only. No code files were created or modified — the outputs consist solely of analysis and reporting documents. The report at line 46 states 'This report was AI-generated by sdlc-workflow/verify-pr' confirming it is a read-only verification report.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md presents findings in a summary table format with verdicts (PASS, MIXED, N/A) and a detailed analysis section. There is no merge action, no merge command, and no auto-merge instruction anywhere in the outputs. The report concludes with analysis and implementation notes for human review.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification verdict appears in the verification report summary table and is accompanied by a detailed analysis section explaining the structural and semantic assessment
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14 contains the summary table row: '| Test Change Classification | MIXED | ...' Lines 33-39 contain the detailed analysis section titled '### Test Change Classification: MIXED' with a 'Structural summary' subsection (lines 34-36) listing function counts and changes per file, and a 'Semantic assessment' subsection (lines 38-39) explaining the rationale. Both structural and semantic assessments are present.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification analysis is based on comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content (function additions, removals, assertion changes) — the structural and semantic assessment references test file content, not acceptance criteria or task requirements (constraint 1.18)
assertion_results.evidence: The structural summary (lines 34-36) explicitly compares base vs PR: 'tests/api/purl_recommend.rs: 4 functions in base, 4 in PR. 1 removed (test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers), 1 added (test_recommend_purls_dedup), 1 modified (test_recommend_purls_basic), 2 unchanged.' and 'Net: 4 base functions -> 7 PR functions (+3 net).' The semantic assessment (lines 38-39) references the removed test function content and its replacement, discussing coverage of removed behavior vs new behavior. The analysis is grounded in file content comparisons (function names, counts, modifications) rather than acceptance criteria.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: '| Test Quality | PASS | All test functions documented; no repetitive tests detected; Eval Quality: N/A |' — Eval Quality is explicitly marked N/A within the Test Quality row. Line 5: '| Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on this PR |' confirms no reviews exist on the PR.
